title: rubus scabridulus
intro: Rubus scabridulus, also known as the rough-leaved bramble or scrub thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's leaves and berries are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Rubus scabridulus can include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
